Container.Name: keyanu
Container.Overview:
Keyanu is a self-hosted infrastructure credential manager for homelabs and system administrators.
This single container includes the web UI, API, SQLite database and Nginx reverse proxy. No additional containers, Docker networks or manual configuration are required.
Before starting the container, generate the required encryption keys.
Generate SECRET_KEY: openssl rand -hex 32
Generate ENCRYPTION_KEY: openssl rand -hex 32
Store both keys in a safe place. Never change ENCRYPTION_KEY after credentials have been created, or all encrypted data will become permanently unreadable.
On first boot, the initial administrator account is created from ADMIN_USERNAME and ADMIN_PASSWORD. You will be required to change the password after the first login.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port used by the Keyanu web interface.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Persistent application data (database, uploaded files and backups).

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: Required. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Never change this key after data has been created.

Config.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Temporary administrator password. You will be prompted to change it after first login.

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Internal application data directory. Leave unchanged unless you also change the Appdata path.

Config.Name: DEFAULT_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES
Config.Description: Default idle session timeout in minutes (720 = 12 hours).
